changeReason: English translation was missing; ENG field contained untranslated Japanese text.

TextENGNew: The Son of God prophesied that he would rise again on the third day.
changeReason: Changes 'promised' to 'prophesied' (JPN 予言 = prophecy, not promise), and 'within three days' to 'on the third day' (JPN 三日の後に = after three days), altering both the nature of the speech act and the timing.

TextENGNew: Sinners sealed the tomb with soldiers and kept watch to ensure his body would not return to life, but this was the height of foolishness.
changeReason: The new translation adds the detail of sealing the tomb with soldiers (JPN 兵にて墓を封じ), which the original omitted entirely, and distinguishes between sealing and watching as separate actions.

TextENGNew: For resurrection does not mean the revival of the flesh--it means the revival of one's spirit and teachings.
changeReason: Changes from a specific attribution ('He meant') to a general philosophical statement about the nature of resurrection, matching the JPN general declarative form (復活とは...だからです).

TextENGNew: Do not fear the death of the body.
If your contributions are unshakeable, you will surely be reborn.
changeReason: Changes the condition from 'achieve great things' to 'contributions are unshakeable' (JPN 貢献が揺ぎ無い = contributions are unwavering), which shifts the criterion from achievement to enduring impact.

TextENGNew: In that moment, you will transcend death and attain eternal life.
And then the devil took the Son of God to the edge of a cliff and told him to leap.
changeReason: Changes 'A demon' to 'the devil' (JPN 悪魔 in biblical context refers to Satan/the devil specifically) and 'son of God' to 'Son of God,' shifting from a generic demon to the specific biblical adversary.

TextENGNew: He claimed that if he was truly the Son of God, then God would perform a miracle to protect him.
changeReason: Changes 'a son of God' to 'the Son of God' (unique title vs generic) and specifies 'God would perform a miracle to protect him' rather than a vague 'a miracle would help him survive,' making God the explicit agent as in the JPN (神は奇跡にてその身を守る).

TextENGNew: To put this to the test is to put God himself to the test.
changeReason: Old misrepresents as narrative action ('He tried to challenge'); Japanese is a philosophical statement about testing God.

TextENGNew: God may test mankind, but mankind must never test God.
changeReason: Changes 'challenge' to 'test' throughout (JPN 試す = to test/try, not challenge), which shifts the meaning from confrontation to verification/doubt.
Changes 'challenge' to 'test' (JPN 試す) and 'suspect' to 'doubt' (JPN 疑う), correcting both key terms to more accurate translations.

TextENGNew: Doubt will lend your ear to the devil's whispers and lead you into corruption.
changeReason: Changes 'Suspicion' to 'Doubt' (JPN 疑い), 'speak words of evil' to 'the devil's whispers' (JPN 悪魔の囁き = devil's whispers), and 'falter' to 'corruption' (JPN 堕落 = corruption/depravity), significantly altering the theological imagery and severity.

TextENGNew: Do not doubt your own achievements.
Do not doubt your own life.
Do not doubt your own contributions.
And do not put others' evaluation of your work to the test.
changeReason: Old mistranslates: says preventing others from criticizing, but Japanese means one must not seek validation.

TextENGNew: For to test that is the same as doubting your very life.
